name: Orville, H. Bissell
motto: "Come then, my friend, my genius, come along; The master of the poet and the song."
description: His musical nature and gentle disposition won him many friends.

name: Titus, Lucretius Bissell
motto: "All sounds on fret by string or golden wire Temper'd soft tunings intermix'd with voice, Choral or unison."
description: "Tite" may enter the telephone business but will design power houses as a pastime.

name: Boggs, LeRoi
motto: His soul was like a star and dwelt apart.
description: After graduation, Boggs will work for the G.E.C. for one month, after which he will edit a county paper at Pickens, S.C.

name: Bond, Stanley Israel
motto: 'Tis remarkable that they talk most who have the least to say.
hometown: Charleston, Bennett, and Beaufort Graded Schools
description: "Cy" will pursue wealth through the cultivation of rice fields near Beaufort.

name: Bristow, Thomas Norwood
motto: "Who could sweetly sing, or with rosin'd bow torment the string."
description: After installing an electric fire alarm system in Bennettsville, Bristow will take charge of the Pendleton Power Plant.

name: Britt, Archer Kennedy
motto: "Beautiful in form and feature, lovely as the day, can there be so fair a creature, formed of common clay?"
description: "Bigun" will some day become Chief Engineer in the construction of "The Japan Canal."

name: Brown, John Jones
motto: Poetic fields encompass me around, and still I seem to tread on classic ground.
description: He will run a cattle ranch at "Cowpens."

name: Campbell, Archibald Lawrence
motto: His sparkling rallies bubbled up as from aerated natural fountains.
description: This young Hercules always took to the side lines in athletics.

name: Berte Dane, Carter
motto: A man who could fight a battle, support a State, or grace a banquet.
description: Little was known of the hamlet of Lodge until it was made famous by his birth. He received his primary education in the Bamberg and Colleton Public Schools. This and his preparatory course at Clemson were so efficient that he stayed at home in 1902 and taught school. He returned to Clemson in 1903 to join the first class of A.I.'s. He won the Debater's medal in his society in 1907. Carter was on the Class football team in Freshman year, guard and tackle on the scrub team in Sophomore and Junior years, and left guard on the famous Varsity team of '06. His present address is care of Chief of Police, Anywhere. He will probably be a fisherman in the Salkahatchie River swamp.

name: John Calvin, Clark
motto: What if it does rain to-morrow. It is fair weather to-day.
clubs: President, South Carolina Collegiate Oratorical Association
description: In no way connected with the character in the Legend of Sleepy Hollow, but a product of Marion County. He began his education in the public schools of the county but, being ambitious, entered Clemson in September 1901 and has since won distinction in the Animal Industry Course. He played right guard on Class football team '05-'06.

name: Francis, Ambrose Connor
motto: "No man is the wiser for his learning; Wit and wisdom are born with a man."
description: Will in the near future erect a trolley line in the town of Greenwood.

name: Edward, Alexander Crawford
motto: "I was not born for courts or great affairs; I pay my debts, believe, and say my prayers."
description: He has done a great deal of research work on the subject of Angora goats. In his Junior year he astonished the scientific world by discovering "nervine in oats" and "perforated holes in rocks." He played Class football in 1904, 1905 and 1906. "Alex," being very fond of goats and dairying, took the course in Animal Industry, and will probably devote his time to the scientific study of "butters."

name: Henry Cecil, Crum
motto: Though matrimony has some pains, Celibacy has few pleasures.
description: Will get married and farm near Denmark.

name: George Dave Curtis
motto: He was made up of love and charms; Whatever maid could wish or man admire.
description: He will be chief engineer for the Anderson Rapid Transit Company.

name: Dalton, Robert Ernest
motto: The pain of one maiden's refusal is drowned in the pain of the next.
description: "Prep" will some day in the near future gain for himself a world-wide reputation in the construction of the Panama Canal.

name: Durant, Charles Edgar
motto: Think not silence the wisdom of fools.
description: This young stryxman was born with a compass in his hand, and after completing the work at the Cottageville High School, his needle pointed him to Clemson, where he entered in 1902. "Dury" followed the course in Civil Engineering, and he has rapidly risen, not only in his chosen profession, but also in the esteem of his fellow classmates. Although a man of but few words, he was always pleasant and companionable.

name: Frank, Marion Dwight
motto: The proverb holds, that to be wise and love Is hardly granted to the Gods above.
description: He will be Chief Electrician at Wedgefield, S.C.

name: Thomas, Dotterer Eason
motto: Give not thy tongue too great liberty, Lest it take thee a prisoner.
clubs: Palmetto Society (Censor, Secretary, Literary Critic, President)
description: He will be a great botanist and a rival of Luther Burbank in propagating new species of plants.

name: Easterling, Robert Addison
motto: I would forget her, but like a fever, she reigns in my blood, and will remembered be.
description: "Bob" will seek his fortune endeavoring to invent some means by which he can procure high starting torque for large capacity motors.

name: Folk, George Hamilton
motto: I consider him my friend who makes me do as much as I can.
description: "Hamp" likes the country and the farm. He will go back to Pomaria and become a soil tiller. No doubt he will add many luxuries to his home through the development of a nearby water power.

name: Fritz, Maholmes Furtick
motto: His life was gentle and the elements so mix'd in him that nature might stand up and say to all the world 'This is a man!'
description: He immediately began his athletic career, and has acquired the reputation of being one of the best athletes in Clemson's history. He made sub-half on Varsity in his first year and since that time has played right-half regularly, not missing a game in four years. Captain of the '06 team. Member of the Track team since '04, and Captain of the '06 and '07 teams. Class President for four years. Member of Columbian Society, in which he was Reporting Critic, Prosecuting Critic and Vice-President; Senior Dancing Club; German Club; '06 Hop Committee. Chief Marshal '06 Commencement.

name: Hugh, Elmore Giles
motto: It ain't no use to grumble and complain, it's jest as cheap an' easy to rejoice.
description: Though "Tubbie" has done well in the course of Electrical Engineering, he will be a country preacher; he will be the pastor of five churches.

name: Godley, John Rice
motto: Silence has become his mother tongue.
description: He was forced to miss the session of 1902-03, but returned at the beginning of the next. He received his early education in the public schools of that county and is still a resident of Colleton County.

name: Harris, Richard Gray
motto: His corn and cattle were his only care, and his chief delight a county fair.
description: As he had no military aspirations, he was a member of the Hospital Corps, commonly known as the "Horse Marines". "Walhalla" has taken a great interest in dairying, and when he leaves Clemson, he will run a cooperative creamery in Laurens County. He received his early education at Wallace Lodge.

name: Keel, Josiah Walker
motto: "Maussa, you means dat niggah Rastus, he's de triflinest, ugliest niggah—but I likes him."
description: Rastus became a disturber of the peace at Allendale, S.C., on January 8th, 1886. Presumably he was sent to cause the good people of that quiet little village to break their New Year's resolutions; and the old inhabitants say that he was a howling success. Some go so far as to say that he was one of the causes of the earthquake that occurred later in the same year, but there is some doubt about this. He attributes his present high standard of scholarship to the foundations laid in the public schools near Allendale. Entered Clemson in 1902, but stayed at home the next year. Returned in 1904, and cast his lot with the straight Agriculturals. Rastus will do errands for the ladies at Allendale.

name: Klugh, Arthur Milwee
motto: "To be glad of life because it gives you a chance to love, and to work, and to play, and to look up at the stars."
clubs: Secretary and Treasurer, Senior Dancing Club
description: Klugh's first achievement will be the winning of a heart.

name: Latimer, William Alexander
motto: "If wit so much from ignorance undergo, Ah, let not learning to commence its foe!"
description: "Bill" will win fame and wealth by surveying and building a railroad over the Blue Ridge Mountains.

name: Lemmon, Robert Howe
motto: "Seest thou a man diligent in his business. He shall stand before kings."

name: Lewis, Harry Gasque
motto: Beauty, and youth, and sprightly hope.
description: He aspires to a "V.-C." position, but his lot will ultimately be a Chair of Agriculture in some A. and M. College.

name: Lykes, Henry Powell
motto: He hath a kind of honor sets him off.
hometown: Winnsboro, later moved to Lykesland, Richland County
description: Played fullback on Class team, 1905; left end on Varsity, 1905 and 1906. Right field of Varsity baseball team, 1906 and 1907. Held the offices of Prosecuting Critic and Reporting Critic in the Columbian Society. Chairman of the Morals Committee and Social Committee. Literary Editor of Annual Staff, Athletic Editor, Tiger Staff.

name: Mack, Cecil Walker
motto: "But see how oft ambitious aims are crossed, And chiefs contend till all the prize is lost."
clubs: Editor, Exchange Department of the Chronicle
description: He will win fame by building a modern electric street railway at Cordova.

name: Mccutcheon, Edward Douglass
motto: "Forthwith from the dance, we proceed to more pleasant repasts."
description: He will be an Electrical Engineer located somewhere near Atlanta.

name: McLendon, James Ward
motto: Happy am I, from care I am free, Why aren't they all contented like me.
description: Behold! Another George Washington! Known to ornithologists as "Mac" and to Prof. Furman as "Our Fat Friend." He was born February 17, 1885, at Timmonsville, S.C., where his early youth was spent raising chickens, and he still holds that there is no higher authority on this subject than himself. We suppose his present ability to tell wonderful stories was gained through long practice in lauding the merits of his feathered pets to the natives of Timmonsville. "Mac" received his early education at the Timmonsville Public and Private Schools, made a flying trip to Welsh Neck High School, entered Clemson September 10th, 1902, and selected the Agricultural and Animal Industry Course. He was a member of the Columbian Literary Society, serving as Prosecuting Critic, Reporting Critic, Secretary, and President. He was Local Editor for the '07 Annual, and a member of the German Club, Senior Dancing Club, and '07 Tennis Club. "Mac" will probably be a patent medicine salesman, with a small comedy company as a side issue, and poultry raising as a pastime.

name: Moore, Henry Walter
motto: The critic eye that microscope of wit, Sees hairs and pores, examines bit by bit.
description: "Judge" or "Corporation" discovered this world at the old historic town of Pendleton, S.C., January 23rd, 1878, but has since journeyed into Oconee County and settled at Okeway. His early education was drilled into him from a blue-back speller at a country schoolhouse in Oconee County. "Judge" entered the ranks of the Freshman Class at Clemson in September 1902 but was absent during the 1903-1904 session. He returned in September 1904 and resumed the scrap for a sheepskin with even more ardor than before. He was a member of the Senior Science Club, serving as Secretary and Treasurer. A dedicated worker in the Columbian Society, he held the offices of Literary Critic and Treasurer. "Judge" will return to the red and rugged old hills of Oconee County and revolutionize the dairy industry of that section by establishing a Cooperative Creamery.

name: Spencer, Rice Perrin
motto: "Oh! bless'd with temper whose unclouded ray will make to-morrow as cheerful as to-day."
clubs: Palmetto Literary Society, Censor, Treasurer, Secretary, President
description: This good-natured, brown-eyed cadet began to train his voice in Union, S.C., September 3rd, 1885, but never had the pleasure of putting it to any official service until appointed Adjutant of the Corps of Cadets at Clemson. He received his early education in the Union Graded Schools, where he probably did well, judging from what he has accomplished since he entered Clemson on September 5th, 1903, for he was easily the leader of his section. Spencer has been fortunate enough to hold offices in nearly everything he entered: marcher of his section, President of the German Club, Manager of the '07 Tennis Club, member of the Senior Dancing Club. He has held the offices of Censor, Treasurer, Secretary and President of the Palmetto Literary Society, and served on the Hop Committee in 1906. Spencer says he is strictly a Union man, therefore a firm believer in the ten-hour day. As he is a Textile student, he will probably spin yarns to the factory maids in Union, and devote his spare time to the completion of his greatest literary production, a one-act comedy entitled "What Steve Saw at the Fair."

name: Daniel, Byrd Peurifoy
motto: "Orators may grieve, for their faculty abides not in their heads, but in their sides."
clubs: Columbian Literary Society, Literary Critic, President
description: He received his early education at the Walterboro and Greenbrier Graded Schools, and entered Clemson in September 1903, pursuing the Animal Industry Course. He joined the Columbian Literary Society and held the offices of Literary Critic and President. He won the Orator's Medal in 1906 and the Trustee's Medal awarded in June 1906. He served as Local Editor of "The Tiger" and was a member of the Senior Dancing Club.

name: Plenge, Edward Bollman
motto: Few words, but to effect.
description: Plenge will some day astonish his friends by working a problem wrong.

name: Pottinger, Charles Theodore
motto: What studies please, what most delight, And fill men's thoughts, they dream them o'er at night.
clubs: Member, Art Staff of the 1906 Annual
Chief Art Editor, staff of the 1907 Annual
description: Although "Theo" has artistic inclinations, yet he will never give up Electricity; for he will soon take charge of the Savannah Electric Company, whose Board of Directors has been anxiously awaiting his graduation.

name: Paul, Quattlebaum
motto: Agree if you can, if not, dispute it like a man.
description: He received his early education at Burroughs Graded School and entered Clemson on September 5, 1903, enrolling in the Electrical Course. He was a member of the Calhoun Society, holding various offices including Sergeant-at-Arms, Chaplain, Treasurer, Critic, and President. During summer 1906, he revised the society's constitution.

name: Obie, M. Reid
motto: Charms strike the eye, but merit wins the soul.
description: Recognizing the town's limitations for his growing potential, he later moved to Anderson city. His foundational education came from Moffettsville Academy and Anderson Graded School. He joined Clemson in September 1903 as part of the Electrical Course cohort, dedicating himself to mastering electrical engineering principles since enrollment.

name: Samuel, Fraser Reid
motto: Fix your eye upon excellence.
description: His early education was received in the public schools of Abbeville County. About this time, being unable to learn to spell the name of his native town, he moved to Abbeville. Finding trouble with that name also, he became discouraged and came to Clemson in 1902. He took the Agricultural Course with special attention to Chemistry. Sam played star ball on the class teams of '05 and '06.

name: Samuel, Roseborough Rhodes
motto: Of all men, respected and admired.
description: His early education was received at the Florence Graded School. When still quite a lad, he entered Furman and, after a four-year course, was graduated with honors. Sam then began teaching, but feeling destined for greater things than teaching young America the A.B.C.'s, he decided to take up engineering; so he joined the Class of '07 in September '05 and signed up for the Electrical Course. Beginning as a special, he held no military offices. Sam will rise as high professionally as he has risen physically.

name: Richardson, Rufus Gordon
motto: All's well that ends well.
clubs: Side Line Artist, all athletic games
description: "R. G." will always advise you to "hold your point."

name: Sanders, George DuPré
motto: A little joy, a little sorrow—whithal a quiet uneventful life.
description: George will manufacture asbestos tapers.

name: Eaton, Love Saunders
motto: These should be hours for necessities, not for delights.
description: Known for his retiring disposition, it's said he will likely "court by correspondence and marry by proxy."

name: William, Leon Schachte
motto: Speak well of everyone if you speak of them at all - no one of us is so very good.
hometown: Charleston, a city noted for its warmth, beauty, culture and style
description: Though not destined to be a great orator, he was known to plead effectively "with a little friend of his."

name: Schirmer, Francis Bonnell
motto: "There's a divinity that shapes our ends, rough-hew them how we will."
description: "Mooney" may one day be City Engineer.

name: Shingler, Edward Marshall
motto: "If music be the food of love, play on."
description: Will sit on the "Battery" 'til 9:30 P.M. waiting for the moon to "rise."

name: Whitfield, William Wannamaker
motto: "Lord God of Hosts be with us yet, lest we forget, lest we forget."
description: "Sergeant" will marry money and spend the rest of his life studying Shakespeare.

name: Lawrence, Stafford Wolfe
motto: "Youth on the prow, and pleasure at the helm."
description: He will develop the economical possibilities of the Bufolentiginosus Americanus (toad).

name: Wood, Oliver Dewitt
motto: I have sworn not to be awed by the pomp of power nor the glamor of ceremony.
description: Wood will organize and manage a baseball team at Greers.

name: Wylie, William Harry Jr.
motto: Aim high and consider yourself capable of great things.
description: He will organize and control a hydraulic electric power plant.
